title: trifolium arvense subsp. preslianum
intro: Trifolium arvense subsp. preslianum, also known as a sub-species of the wild clover, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the legume family. The plant has been found to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Trifolium arvense subsp. preslianum include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against infections. The herbal preparations of Trifolium arvense subsp. preslianum are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
